claim_text: AI-driven child sexual exploitation poses a new frontier of threats.
query:
Task: Provide up to 25 scientific references relevant to the claim.
Context: California legislative press release analysis.
Claim (20241226-new-year-new-laws.txt / #1): AI-driven child sexual exploitation poses a new frontier of threats.
Constraints:
- Prioritize peer-reviewed sources.
- Include DOI when available.
- Keep abstracts to 1-2 sentence summaries if exact abstracts not available.
- Prefer recency for fast-moving topics (e.g., deepfakes, LLMs), else include seminal works.
- provider = discovery source (e.g., 'arXiv' if link is on arxiv.org; 'publisher' for publisher site);
- venue = actual journal or conference (use 'arXiv' only if there is no published venue).
- Normalize venue names to standard short forms (e.g., NeurIPS, ICML, ICLR, CVPR, ICCV, ECCV, AAAI, IJCAI, ACL, EMNLP, NAACL).
Output: JSON object {"references": [{provider,id,doi,title,abstract,year,venue,authors,url,is_open_access}...]}.

references.title: Rising Threats of AI-Driven Child Sexual Abuse Material
references.abstract: Perspective outlining how generative AI enables creation and spread of virtual CSAM, complicating investigations and posing clinical, legal, and policy challenges for protecting children.

references.title: Artificial intelligence and child sexual abuse: A rapid evidence assessment
references.abstract: Peer‑reviewed government report synthesizing empirical research on how AI is used to perpetrate and to combat child sexual abuse, highlighting gaps and research priorities.
references.venue: Trends & Issues in Crime and Criminal Justice

references.title: Threat of deepfakes to the criminal justice system: a systematic review
references.abstract: Systematic review mapping how AI‑generated synthetic media, including sexual deepfakes, threaten investigations, evidence integrity, and prosecution, and summarizing mitigation strategies.

references.title: Deepfakes and Digitally Altered Imagery Abuse: A Cross-Country Exploration of an Emerging form of Image-Based Sexual Abuse
references.abstract: Empirical study across the UK, New Zealand, and Australia showing how deepfake and altered sexual imagery constitute a serious, growing form of image‑based abuse with policy and policing challenges.

references.title: The Characteristics of Virtual Child Sexual Abuse Material Offenders and the Harms of Offending: A Qualitative Content Analysis of Print Media
references.abstract: Qualitative analysis of media reports on virtual CSAM offending, describing offender characteristics, harms, and narratives around legality and risk escalation.

references.title: A deep learning framework for finding illicit images/videos of children
references.abstract: Presents and evaluates a deep learning pipeline for identifying illicit images and videos of children to aid digital forensics and triage.

references.title: Detecting sexually explicit content in the context of the child sexual abuse materials (CSAM): end-to-end classifiers and region-based networks
references.abstract: Explores end‑to‑end and region‑based deep models for sexually explicit content detection as a component of automated CSAM moderation under stringent data‑access constraints.

references.title: Leveraging Self-Supervised Learning for Scene Classification in Child Sexual Abuse Imagery
references.abstract: Investigates self‑supervised scene classification to assist CSAM investigations without training directly on sensitive content, reporting performance and domain shift issues.

references.title: Contrastive Language-Vision AI Models Pretrained on Web-Scraped Multimodal Data Exhibit Sexual Objectification Bias
references.abstract: Shows that popular multimodal and generative models can produce sexualized images from prompts such as “a [age]-year-old girl,” underscoring risks of AI systems sexualizing minors.

references.title: Generative ML and CSAM: Implications and Mitigations
references.abstract: Joint report analyzes how generative image models enable creation and distribution of realistic CSAM and proposes mitigation steps across the model and platform lifecycle.

references.title: Identifying and Eliminating CSAM in Generative ML Training Data and Models
references.abstract: Technical report documents thousands of CSAM links in a major image‑text dataset used to train popular generative models, with recommendations for dataset cleansing and model deprecation.

references.title: Non-Consensual Synthetic Intimate Imagery: Prevalence, Attitudes, and Knowledge in 10 Countries
references.abstract: Large cross‑national survey quantifies victimization and perpetration of deepfake pornography and attitudes towards it, informing policy on synthetic sexual imagery harms.

references.title: Evolving from Single-modal to Multi-modal Facial Deepfake Detection: A Survey
references.abstract: Survey of modern deepfake generation and detection, including diffusion‑based methods and multi‑modal detection strategies relevant to combating sexualized synthetic media.

references.title: Effectiveness of machine learning methods in detecting grooming: a systematic meta-analytic review
references.abstract: Meta‑analysis of ML approaches for online grooming detection reports strong performance for several models, supporting AI use in early identification of child exploitation risks.

references.title: Applying Artificial Intelligence for Age Estimation in Digital Forensic Investigations
references.abstract: Discusses AI‑based facial age estimation to assist investigations where apparent age is critical, highlighting dataset needs and forensic considerations.

references.title: ChildDiffusion: Unlocking the Potential of Generative AI and Controllable Augmentations for Child Facial Data using Stable Diffusion and Large Language Models
references.abstract: Introduces a framework for generating photorealistic child faces and augmentations; while aimed at benign datasets, it illustrates capabilities that could be misused for synthetic child imagery.

references.title: A Contemporary Survey on Deepfake Detection: Datasets, Algorithms, and Challenges
references.abstract: Comprehensive review of deepfake detection methods and datasets, including issues introduced by diffusion models relevant to sexual deepfake mitigation.

references.title: Child sexual abuse material on the darknet
references.abstract: Reviews the prevalence, dynamics, and policing of CSAM on darknet markets and forums, providing context for how AI‑generated content may intersect with existing ecosystems.

references.title: Guarding the Guardians: Automated Analysis of Online Child Sexual Abuse
references.abstract: Proposes an automated NLP pipeline to categorize online child sexual abuse reports, aiming to reduce analyst exposure and support law‑enforcement triage.

references.title: Livestreaming Technology and Online Child Sexual Exploitation and Abuse: A Scoping Review
references.abstract: Scoping review of livestreamed child sexual exploitation and abuse, discussing technology‑facilitated dynamics and implications for prevention and response.

query:
Task: Provide up to 25 scientific references relevant to the claim.
Context: California legislative press release analysis.
Claim (20241226-new-year-new-laws.txt / #2): AI-generated elections deepfakes pose threats.
Constraints:
- Prioritize peer-reviewed sources.
- Include DOI when available.
- Keep abstracts to 1-2 sentence summaries if exact abstracts not available.
- Prefer recency for fast-moving topics (e.g., deepfakes, LLMs), else include seminal works.
- provider = discovery source (e.g., 'arXiv' if link is on arxiv.org; 'publisher' for publisher site);
- venue = actual journal or conference (use 'arXiv' only if there is no published venue).
- Normalize venue names to standard short forms (e.g., NeurIPS, ICML, ICLR, CVPR, ICCV, ECCV, AAAI, IJCAI, ACL, EMNLP, NAACL).
Output: JSON object {"references": [{provider,id,doi,title,abstract,year,venue,authors,url,is_open_access}...]}.

references.title: Deepfakes and Disinformation: Exploring the Impact of Synthetic Political Video on Deception, Uncertainty, and Trust in News
references.abstract: An online experiment with a representative UK sample shows political deepfakes are more likely to induce uncertainty than outright deception, but this uncertainty reduces trust in news on social media.

references.title: Do (Microtargeted) Deepfakes Have Real Effects on Political Attitudes?
references.abstract: Using a political deepfake in an experiment, the authors find small negative effects on attitudes toward the depicted politician; microtargeting can amplify effects for a narrow susceptible subgroup.

references.title: They Would Never Say Anything Like This! Reasons To Doubt Political Deepfakes
references.abstract: An experiment shows people find political deepfakes relatively believable and struggle to distinguish them from authentic videos; doubts hinge more on content implausibility than on visible glitches.
authors: Toni G. L. A. van der Meer

references.title: Political deepfakes are as credible as other fake media and (sometimes) real media
references.abstract: Across large-scale experiments with realistic political deepfakes, the authors find deepfakes are about as credible as textual/audio misinformation; exposure can also prompt mislabeling of authentic media as fake.

references.title: The Liar’s Dividend: Can Politicians Claim Misinformation to Evade Accountability?
references.abstract: Five survey experiments (N>15,000) show politicians can boost support by falsely claiming stories are fake news or deepfakes, especially for text-based scandals—highlighting indirect threats to accountability.

references.title: Deepfakes and Democracy (Theory): How Synthetic Audio-Visual Media for Disinformation and Hate Speech Threaten Core Democratic Functions
references.abstract: This theory article argues deepfakes undermine empowered inclusion, deliberation, and collective decision-making by degrading trust and the epistemic quality of public discourse—threatening elections even via fear effects.

references.title: On the way to deep fake democracy? Deep fakes in election campaigns in 2023
references.abstract: A cross-country analysis of 2023 election cases documents political use of deepfakes and argues their main current danger is polluting the information environment and eroding trust ahead of major 2024 elections.

references.title: Fooled twice: People cannot detect deepfakes but think they can
references.abstract: A preregistered experiment finds individuals perform poorly at detecting deepfakes, remain biased toward believing fakes are real, and overestimate their detection ability—implications for susceptibility during elections.

references.title: Deepfake detection by human crowds, machines, and machine-informed crowds
references.abstract: With 15,016 participants, the study shows ordinary humans and leading models achieve similar deepfake-detection accuracy; combining them helps, but incorrect model cues can mislead humans—relevant for election integrity workflows.

references.title: Human detection of political speech deepfakes across transcripts, audio, and video
references.abstract: Across five preregistered experiments, audio-visual cues improve discernment relative to text alone, and TTS-based audio deepfakes are harder to detect than voice-actor versions—highlighting risks from realistic synthetic speech in politics.

references.title: Detection of political deepfakes
references.abstract: Three preregistered studies on political deepfakes (e.g., Johnson, Obama) propose a detection theory and show analytic thinking and political interest modestly improve human detection of manipulated videos.

references.title: Seeing Is Believing: Is Video Modality More Powerful in Spreading Fake News via Online Messaging Apps?
references.abstract: A field experiment in India finds video misinformation is perceived as more credible and is more likely to be shared than audio or text—implicating deepfake videos as potent vectors during elections.

references.title: The Epistemic Threat of Deepfakes
references.abstract: Analyzes how deepfakes reduce the information-carrying value of video evidence, thereby threatening knowledge practices and public accountability central to democratic elections.

references.title: The Distinct Wrong of Deepfakes
references.abstract: Argues deepfakes enable new forms of deception—with societal risks including blackmail, incitement, and democratic harm—and evaluates mitigation like labeling and provenance with limits for election contexts.

references.title: Deep Fakes: A Looming Challenge for Privacy, Democracy, and National Security
references.abstract: Seminal legal analysis detailing how deepfakes can distort democratic discourse, manipulate elections, and erode institutional trust; introduces the “liar’s dividend.”

references.title: Anticipating and addressing the ethical implications of deepfakes in the context of elections
references.abstract: Using scenario analysis, the authors outline election-specific harms from deepfakes and recommend responses spanning literacy, subject defense, verification, and publicity modulation.

references.title: Protecting Democracy from Disinformation: Normative Threats and Policy Responses
references.abstract: Conceptualizes how disinformation threatens democratic goods including electoral integrity and deliberation, clarifying policy levers that also apply to deepfake-based election manipulation.

references.title: Cortical-striatal brain network distinguishes deepfake from real speaker identity
references.abstract: Neuroimaging evidence shows human brain networks differentially process deepfake vs. real voices, underscoring why audio deepfakes can mislead perception—relevant to robocall-style election threats.

references.title: What we know and don’t know about deepfakes: An investigation into the state of the research and regulatory landscape
references.abstract: A 2024 review synthesizes evidence on deepfake prevalence, effects, and governance; it notes limited persuasive advantage but significant risks via uncertainty and trust erosion in political contexts.

references.title: Deepfakes and the Epistemic Backstop
references.abstract: Philosophical analysis arguing that as recordings lose their ‘epistemic backstop’ role due to deepfakes, testimonial trust and public accountability—crucial in elections—are endangered.

references.title: Deepfakes and the 2020 US elections: what (did not) happen
references.abstract: This analysis contends that warnings, platform actions, and awareness helped prevent deepfakes from affecting the 2020 U.S. elections, while cautioning that conditions may differ in future contests.

claim_text: AI-generated pictures and videos of children being sexually abused are disturbing, harmful, and virtually indistinguishable from actual children.
query:
Task: Provide up to 25 scientific references relevant to the claim.
Context: California legislative press release analysis.
Claim (20241226-new-year-new-laws.txt / #3): AI-generated pictures and videos of children being sexually abused are disturbing, harmful, and virtually indistinguishable from actual children.
Constraints:
- Prioritize peer-reviewed sources.
- Include DOI when available.
- Keep abstracts to 1-2 sentence summaries if exact abstracts not available.
- Prefer recency for fast-moving topics (e.g., deepfakes, LLMs), else include seminal works.
- provider = discovery source (e.g., 'arXiv' if link is on arxiv.org; 'publisher' for publisher site);
- venue = actual journal or conference (use 'arXiv' only if there is no published venue).
- Normalize venue names to standard short forms (e.g., NeurIPS, ICML, ICLR, CVPR, ICCV, ECCV, AAAI, IJCAI, ACL, EMNLP, NAACL).
Output: JSON object {"references": [{provider,id,doi,title,abstract,year,venue,authors,url,is_open_access}...]}.

references.title: AI-synthesized faces are indistinguishable from real faces and more trustworthy
references.abstract: Across perceptual experiments, participants could not reliably distinguish GAN-synthesized faces from real ones and rated synthetic faces as slightly more trustworthy, indicating high photorealism that spans gender, race, and age ranges.

references.title: Testing human ability to detect ‘deepfake’ images of human faces
references.abstract: In an online experiment (N=280), average human accuracy for distinguishing StyleGAN2-generated faces from real faces was about 60–64% with high but misplaced confidence, and simple training interventions did not substantially improve performance.

references.title: Interpretation of AI-Generated vs. Human-Made Images
references.abstract: With 161 participants (professionals and non-professionals), AI-generated images were frequently misclassified as human-made and judged comparably credible, underscoring difficulties of human provenance detection in realistic imagery.

references.title: Seeing is not always believing: Benchmarking Human and Model Perception of AI-Generated Images
references.abstract: A large-scale benchmark finds that humans misclassify AI-generated images at high rates (38.7% error), while detectors still face generalization challenges, highlighting how photorealistic synthesis undermines human judgment.

references.title: Prevalence of Online Sexual Offenses Against Children in the US
references.abstract: A national survey (N=2,639) estimates lifetime exposure among youth to image-based sexual abuse at 11% and sextortion at 3.5%, evidencing the scale and harms of technology-facilitated sexual victimization of minors.

references.title: Exposure to sexual content and problematic sexual behaviors in children and adolescents: A systematic review and meta-analysis
references.abstract: Meta-analysis of 27 studies (N≈16,200) finds that exposure to sexual content (including online pornography) is significantly associated with increased odds of problematic sexual behaviors in children and adolescents.

references.title: Which dynamics make online child sexual abuse and cyberstalking more emotionally impactful: Perpetrator identity and images?
references.abstract: Using detailed episode data from U.S. young adults recalling incidents before age 18, the study shows that sexual image misuse (non-consensual sharing/taking and sextortion) is linked to higher negative emotional impact.

references.title: Sextortion: A Scoping Review
references.abstract: A scoping review of 24 studies reports youth sextortion victimization prevalence of roughly 0.7–5% and documents substantial psychological and social harms, underscoring the seriousness of technology-facilitated sexual coercion.

references.title: Outcomes of image-based sexual abuse among young people: a systematic review
references.abstract: Synthesizing 12 empirical studies on individuals aged 10–24, the review finds IBSA is associated with severe emotional distress (e.g., anxiety, depression, suicidality) and social/educational harms.

references.title: Assessing image-based sexual abuse: Measurement, prevalence, and temporal stability of sextortion and nonconsensual sexting (“revenge porn”) among adolescents
references.abstract: Among 1,820 adolescents, validated measures captured sextortion and nonconsensual sexting; victimization correlated with depression and anxiety, and showed notable one-year stability, highlighting harm in youth.

references.title: Contrastive Language-Vision AI Models Pretrained on Web-Scraped Multimodal Data Exhibit Sexual Objectification Bias
references.abstract: Evaluating CLIP-class models and text-to-image systems, the study finds sexual objectification biases and shows that prompts like "a [age]-year-old girl" can yield sexualized images, evidencing risks for generating sexualized depictions of minors.

references.title: Deepfake Detection: A Comprehensive Survey from the Reliability Perspective
references.abstract: A comprehensive survey of deepfake detection highlights persistent challenges in transferability, robustness, and interpretability, indicating current detectors often fail to generalize across generators and conditions.

references.title: Online Detection of AI-Generated Images
references.abstract: Presents a streaming setting for AI-image detection aligned to real-world generator releases and extends to pixel-level detection for partial manipulations, emphasizing generalization issues for evolving synthesis.

references.title: PatchCraft: Exploring Texture Patch for Efficient AI-generated Image Detection
references.abstract: Proposes a texture-focused detector that improves cross-model detection of AI-generated images and notes that humans can hardly distinguish highly realistic AI imagery, motivating stronger forensic tools.

references.title: Learning to Break Deep Perceptual Hashing: The Use Case NeuralHash
references.abstract: Shows that Apple's NeuralHash can be reliably evaded or manipulated with small perturbations, raising concerns about client-side CSAM detection robustness against adversaries.

references.title: Sexual abuse and post-traumatic stress disorder in childhood, adolescence and young adulthood: a systematic review and meta-analysis
references.abstract: Across 28 studies (N≈28,693), child sexual abuse is strongly associated with PTSD in youth regardless of gender, underscoring severe psychological harms from sexual victimization.

references.title: Non-Consensual Synthetic Intimate Imagery: Prevalence, Attitudes, and Knowledge in 10 Countries
references.abstract: A cross-national survey of >16,000 respondents finds 2.2% victimization and 1.8% self-reported perpetration of deepfake pornography, with broad recognition of harm and limited deterrence from current laws.

claim_text: Malicious deepfake audio or visual media can falsely portray the actions or words of a candidate or elected official in the lead up to an election.
query:
Task: Provide up to 25 scientific references relevant to the claim.
Context: California legislative press release analysis.
Claim (20241226-new-year-new-laws.txt / #5): Malicious deepfake audio or visual media can falsely portray the actions or words of a candidate or elected official in the lead up to an election.
Constraints:
- Prioritize peer-reviewed sources.
- Include DOI when available.
- Keep abstracts to 1-2 sentence summaries if exact abstracts not available.
- Prefer recency for fast-moving topics (e.g., deepfakes, LLMs), else include seminal works.
- provider = discovery source (e.g., 'arXiv' if link is on arxiv.org; 'publisher' for publisher site);
- venue = actual journal or conference (use 'arXiv' only if there is no published venue).
- Normalize venue names to standard short forms (e.g., NeurIPS, ICML, ICLR, CVPR, ICCV, ECCV, AAAI, IJCAI, ACL, EMNLP, NAACL).

references.title: Deepfakes and Disinformation: Exploring the Impact of Synthetic Political Video on Deception, Uncertainty, and Trust in News
references.abstract: An experiment with a representative UK sample finds political deepfakes are more likely to induce uncertainty than outright deception, and this uncertainty erodes trust in news on social media.

references.title: Do (Microtargeted) Deepfakes Have Real Effects on Political Attitudes?
references.abstract: Using a constructed political deepfake in an online experiment, the authors show attitudes toward the depicted politician become more negative; effects can be amplified by microtargeting for a small susceptible subgroup.

references.title: The (minimal) persuasive advantage of political video over text
references.abstract: Across large-scale experiments, video increases belief relative to text but yields only small persuasion gains, tempering expectations that video deepfakes are uniquely persuasive compared to other media.

references.title: Human detection of political speech deepfakes across transcripts, audio, and video
references.abstract: Five preregistered experiments show that deepfakes using state-of-the-art text-to-speech are harder to discern than those with voice actors, and that audiovisual cues improve human discernment over text alone.

references.title: AI-synthesized faces are indistinguishable from real faces and more trustworthy
references.abstract: Human participants perform at chance in distinguishing AI-synthesized from real faces, and synthetic faces are rated as more trustworthy, underscoring risks of deception from realistic generative media.

references.title: Warning: Humans cannot reliably detect speech deepfakes
references.abstract: In tests with 529 participants across English and Mandarin, listeners identified deepfake speech only about 73% of the time, with limited improvement after training, highlighting susceptibility to audio impersonation.

references.title: Face2Face: Real-Time Face Capture and Reenactment of RGB Videos
references.abstract: Introduces a real-time facial reenactment system that transfers expressions from a source to a target video, enabling photorealistic manipulation of a person's facial movements and speech articulation.

references.title: Synthesizing Obama: Learning Lip Sync from Audio
references.abstract: Presents a method that, given audio of President Barack Obama, synthesizes high-quality video with accurate lip-sync, demonstrating the feasibility of altering what a public figure appears to say.

references.title: FaceForensics++: Learning to Detect Manipulated Facial Images
references.abstract: Releases a large-scale benchmark of manipulated face videos and shows that specialized detection models can outperform humans, standardizing evaluation for deepfake detection.

references.title: The detection of political deepfakes
references.abstract: Across preregistered studies with deepfakes of prominent politicians, analytic thinking and political interest predict better identification; results illuminate human detection challenges for political deepfakes.

references.title: Political deepfakes are as credible as other fake media and (sometimes) real media
references.abstract: Experiments indicate political deepfakes can be as credible as text or audio misinformation and, in some contexts, approach the credibility of real media, while also fostering misclassification of authentic videos.

references.title: On the way to deep fake democracy? Deep fakes in election campaigns in 2023
references.abstract: A cross-country analysis documents how deepfakes were used around elections in 2023, assessing implications for electoral processes and democratic trust.

references.title: The Malicious Use of Artificial Intelligence: Forecasting, Prevention, and Mitigation
references.abstract: This report surveys near-term malicious uses of AI across digital, physical, and political domains, highlighting threats such as deepfakes and recommending mitigations.

references.title: Neural Voice Cloning with a Few Samples
references.abstract: Demonstrates voice cloning from only a few audio samples via speaker adaptation and speaker encoding, lowering barriers to realistic speech impersonation.

references.title: Neural Codec Language Models are Zero-Shot Text to Speech Synthesizers
references.abstract: Introduces VALL-E, a zero-shot TTS system that can mimic an unseen speaker’s voice from a 3-second sample, illustrating how little data is needed for convincing voice cloning.

references.title: The Liar’s Dividend: Can Politicians Claim Misinformation to Evade Accountability?
references.abstract: Across five survey experiments with over 15,000 US adults, false claims of misinformation boost support for politicians facing text-based scandals but have limited effect against scandals documented on video.

references.title: They Would Never Say Anything Like This! Reasons To Doubt Political Deepfakes
references.abstract: Using custom political deepfakes, this study analyzes why audiences doubt manipulated videos—e.g., incongruent style or implausible statements—informing defenses against political deepfake deception.
authors: Toni G. L. A. van der Meer
